diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 8384e3d..79ad03e 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -253,2 +253,8 @@ Day 245 / Week 35 observations:
 - 131 new enterprise leads arrived with 66,660 seats. Sent the same low-friction pricing: E1 $7/$12/$20, E2 $9/$17/$28, E3 $12/$25/$42.
 - Week 35 adjustment: restore some support to global ops $60k/day and S2 ops $90k/day, and start a temporary targeted enterprise dev push: E1 $40k/day, E2 $80k/day, E3 $30k/day, while keeping S2 dev $18k/day and maintenance S2 content spend. Tier-10 is expected day 268 and should close the remaining quality gap.
+
+Day 252 / Week 36 observations:
+- Cash rose to $1.95M despite the enterprise dev push. Individual subs reached 112,725, open issues improved slightly to 3,665, but cancellations rose to 894.
+- Enterprise targeted dev worked: E1 C quality is now 2.23, E2 C 2.33, and E3 C 2.19. E1/E2 are still just below their level-2 estimated quality floors on tier-4 C.
+- 452 new enterprise leads arrived with 260,071 seats. Sent the same low-friction enterprise pricing.
+- Week 36 adjustment: move plan C to model tier 5 so E1/E2 C quality clears the estimated floors immediately, upgrade capacity to tier 7 before potential enterprise acceptance, and trim enterprise targeted dev to E1 $20k/day, E2 $50k/day, E3 $10k/day while keeping S2 dev and support stable.
